_comment: Token budget per agent role per invocation. Claude enforces these limits. All values in approximate tokens. Tiers stack: tier_0 + tier_1 + tier_2 <= total_cap.

_tiers.tier_0: Agent identity file (including Lessons section) + Current State Header. ALWAYS loaded.
_tiers.tier_1: Current commit spec + relevant handoffs + project-state.json. Loaded per invocation.
_tiers.tier_2: Most recent 2 worklog sessions + specific DECISIONS.md entries. Loaded when historical depth is needed.
_tiers.total_cap: Hard cap per invocation. If context exceeds this, Claude compresses before invoking.

backend.notes: Tier 2 used for: referencing prior schema decisions, checking migration history, resolving service interface questions.

devops.notes: DevOps rarely needs deep history. Tier 2 mainly for: checking prior env var decisions, reviewing startup sequence history.

reviewer.notes: Viktor needs larger tier_2 to read the full diff context and surrounding code. Never needs worklog history — reads diffs only.

security.notes: Sage needs the full changed files plus surrounding context for accurate threat modeling. Attack surface map from prior sessions is tier_2.

qa.notes: Quinn reads changed code + existing test files. Tier_2 for: coverage debt log from prior sessions, known gaps carried forward.

product.notes: Mira reviews Claude's summary, not raw code. Smallest budget — product review is high-level.

frontend.notes: Similar budget to backend. Tier_2 for: reviewing prior API shape decisions, component hierarchy history.

ai-engineer.notes: Slightly larger tier_0 — AI engineer identity includes prompt patterns and tool schema conventions that take more space.

tech-writer.notes: Smallest depth needed. Ryan reads summary + changed API/config. Tier_2 for: consistency check against prior DECISIONS.md entries.

claude.notes: Claude as orchestrator needs more context than individual agents. Reads all Current State Headers + protocol + state file every session.

session_limits.alert_message: Session is at {pct}% of context limit. Claude should compress worklog summaries before the next agent invocation.
session_limits.compress_action: Archive the oldest worklog sessions for each active agent. Rebuild Current State Headers from the compressed state.

archive_protocol.trigger: After every 5 completed sessions per agent
archive_protocol.action: Move sessions 1-N to [agent]-worklog-archive-[batch].md. Compress to 20-line Archive Entry in the active worklog.

lessons_protocol.archive_action: Move oldest 10 lessons to [agent]-lessons-archive.md. Keep 10 most recent in identity file.
lessons_protocol.lessons_token_note: Lessons are part of tier_0. The 2000 token budget is included within each agent tier_0 allocation. If lessons exceed 2000 tokens, trigger archive regardless of entry count.
lessons_protocol.quality_bar: Every lesson must have Trigger + Pattern + Why it matters. Reject generic lessons at write time.
